data.title: Mixed Martial Arts gets $2M non-dilutive financing
data.description:
• Mixed Martial Arts (NYSE:MMA) said it has entered into a non-dilutive and unsecured $2M revolving loan agreement.
  • The loan will accrue interest at a rate of 14% per annum and will mature 12 months from issuance.
  • As part of the financing, Hugh Williams and Jonathan Hart resigned as directors of the firm, and Richard Paolone, Eric Corbett, and Angel Liriano were appointed as directors.

data.title: Morgan Stanley Q1 earnings
data.description:
Morgan Stanley (NYSE:MS) delivered a Q1 earnings beat on Friday as equities revenue surged Y/Y and client assets increased at its wealth and investment management businesses. Pretax profit margin grew to 31% in the quarter.

"Institutional Securities strong performance was led by our Markets business, with Equity reporting a record $4.1B in revenues," Chairman and CEO Ted Pick said. "Total client assets of $7.7T across Wealth and Investment Management were supported by $94B in net new assets."

Morgan Stanley (NYSE:MS) stock rose 0.4% in premarket trading.

Q1 GAAP EPS of $2.60, topping the average analyst estimate of $2.19, rose from $2.22 in Q4 2024 and $2.02 in Q1 2024.

Net revenue of $17.7B, beating the consensus estimate of $16.6B, climbed from $16.2B in the prior quarter and $15.1B a year ago.

Morgan Stanley’s (MS) net interest income of $2.35B, matching the Visible Alpha consensus, decreased from $2.55B in Q4 and increased from $1.80B in last year’s Q1.

Pretax profit margin of 31% vs. 30% in the previous quarter and 29% a year ago. Compensation and benefits, as a percentage of net revenue, was 42% compared with 39% in Q4 and 44% in Q1 2024.

Total non-compensation expenses were $4.54B, down from $4.91B in the prior quarter and up from $4.05B a year ago.

Provision for credit losses of $134M vs. $77M Visible Alpha consensus, rose from $115M in the previous quarter and -$0.6M a year ago.

Morgan Stanley's (MS) return on average tangible common equity of 23.0% strengthened from 20.2% in Q4 2024 and 19.7% in Q1 2024.

Revenue by segment:

  • Institutional Securities: $8.98B jumped 24% Q/Q and 28% Y/Y; investment banking revenue of $1.56B increased 8% Y/Y. Equity net revenue of $4.13B shot up 45% Y/Y, reflecting increases across all business lines and regions, especially Asia. Fixed income net revenue of $2.60B rose 5% Y/Y.

  • Wealth Management: $7.33B slipped 2% Q/Q and rose 6% Y/Y. Asset management revenue of $4.40B climbed from $3.83B a year ago on higher asset levels and the cumulative impact of positive fee-based flows.

  • Investment Management: $1.60B fell 5% Q/Q and 26% Y/Y. Asset management and related fees of $1.45B grew from $1.35B a year ago, on higher average AUM, primarily driven by higher market levels, the company said.

Conference call at 9:30 AM ET.

data.title: Intapp announces plan to acquire TermSheet
data.description:
• Intapp (NASDAQ:INTA) has signed an agreement to acquire TermSheet, a provider of software for real estate teams.
  • TermSheet, is an affiliate of Platform Ventures, a Kansas City-based investment firm. The transaction is expected to close within the next 45 days.
  • Together, Intapp DealCloud and TermSheet will deliver an advanced operating system that applies intelligence, so clients can better execute across the client lifecycle, improve returns, and foster firm growth.
  • “Bringing together Intapp DealCloud and TermSheet will deliver a more powerful operating system tailored to the complex needs of the commercial real estate industry and create an unparalleled team of industry experts.” said Erin Guinan, General Manager of DealCloud at Intapp.

data.title: CSW Industrials raises dividend by 12.5% to $0.27
data.description:
• CSW Industrials (NASDAQ:CSWI) declares $0.27/share quarterly dividend, 12.5% increase from prior dividend of $0.24.
  • Forward yield 0.38%
  • Payable May 9; for shareholders of record April 25; ex-div April 25.
  • See CSWI Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Bit Digital secures rights for data center site to support colocation agreement with Cerebras
data.description:
Bitcoin miner Bit Digital (NASDAQ:BTBT) said on Friday it has secured the rights for a new data center site in Saint-Jérôme, Québec, to support a 5MW colocation agreement with Cerebras Systems.

Shares were trading +2.88% pre-market at $1.78.

The company had announced a colocation contract with a leading AI hardware innovator in February.

The facility spans ~202,000 sq. ft. on 7.7 acres and is being developed to support current contracted capacity, with future expansion potential subject to utility approvals.

data.title: Lithium Argentina, China's Ganfeng to jointly develop Pozuelos-Pastos Grandes basins
data.description:
Lithium Argentina (NYSE:LAR) said Friday it signed a letter of intent with China's Ganfeng Lithium (OTCPK:GNENY) (OTCPK:GNENF) to jointly develop the Pozuelos-Pastos Grandes basins, which includes Ganfeng’s wholly-owned Pozuelos-Pastos Grandes project as well as the jointly-owned Pastos Grandes and Sal de la Puna projects in Argentina.

The companies said the development plan targets a combined capacity of as much as 150K metric tons/year of lithium carbonate equivalent, utilizing solar evaporation and direct lithium extraction technology, and a 5K tons/year DLE demonstration plant is under construction for the jointly managed Cauchari-Olaroz lithium operation, which is expected to be completed later this year.

Lithium Argentina (NYSE:LAR) and Ganfeng (OTCPK:GNENY) (OTCPK:GNENF) said they are jointly exploring financing options for PPG, including collaboration with potential customers and strategic partners for offtake and minority ownership interests.

data.title: Ocean Power signs reseller agreement with Elektron
data.description:
• Ocean Power Technologies (NYSE:OPTT) has announced the signing of a reseller agreement with Elektron SAS, a Cartegena, Colombia based specialist in hydrographic and oceanographic instrumentation services.
  • This partnership significantly expands OPT’s commercial footprint across Latin America, leveraging Elektron’s strong regional presence and deep relationships.
  • Under the agreement, Elektron will offer sales, integration, and support services and includes a $4M purchase commitment for OPT’s full suite of intelligent maritime technologies.
  • “This agreement represents another key step forward in continuing to scale OPT’s global reseller network and unlocking additional high-growth markets,” said Philipp Stratmann, CEO of Ocean Power Technologies.
  • OPTT +15.3% premarket to $0.46.

data.title: UK approves SHEIN IPO, waiting for nod from Beijing - report
data.description:
Chinese fast fashion brand SHEIN was given the okay from British regulators for its initial public offering, but has yet to gain approval from Beijing, according to sources cited by Reuters.

SHEIN initially filed for an IPO last June with the UK Financial Conduct Authority after abandoning a bid to be listed on the NYSE as accusations surrounding the use of slave labor coupled with rigid U.S. regulations made a U.S. IPO more problematic.

The company wasn’t completely out of the woods in the UK, either. Earlier this year, the British parliament summoned representatives from SHEIN and Temu (NASDAQ:PDD) to address accusations of slave labor practices and for the use of banned cotton from Xinjiang, only to be frustrated by executives' evasiveness.

“The reluctance to answer basic questions has frankly bordered on contempt of the committee,” UK committee chair for business and trade Liam Byrne was quoted by The Guardian as saying in response to SHEIN’s unwillingness to answer questions about its manufacturing processes.

More recently, shifts in U.S. trade policy—particularly President Trump’s Executive Order ending the de minimis exemption—and the intensifying trade tensions with China have put SHEIN’s $50B valuation at risk. Combined with ongoing market volatility that complicates any public offering, these factors could further delay the company’s potential listing on the FTSE-100, as well as thwart an approval from Beijing.

data.title: Nvidia, Marvell in focus as Citi lowers estimates on tariff uncertainty
data.description:
Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) and Marvell Technology (NASDAQ:MRVL) were in focus on Friday as Citi lowered its estimates on the pair, citing uncertainty about tariffs.

“We are lowering our GPU units for CY25/26 by 3%/5% to align with our revised hyperscaler capex model of +35%/15% spend reflecting mostly lower [Microsoft (MSFT)] capex concerns and higher risk of pause in enterprise investments amid uncertainty around the global economy due to ongoing trade war,” analyst Atif Malik wrote in a note about Nvidia.

Amid the trade war, Nvidia could “partially” pass down the increased GPU cost to customers, but it will likely impact margins to some extent. As such, Malik lowered his earnings estimates for 2025 and 2026 by 3% and 6%, respectively and cut his price target to $150 but kept his Buy rating.

Malik also lowered his revenue and earnings estimates on Marvell for fiscal 2027 by 5% and 8%, respectively, due to the worries about hyperscaler spending and the tariff cuts.

“More specifically, we cut our MRVL ASIC assumptions for FY2027 by 20% amid the C2026 [chip-on-a-wafer-substrate] estimates revisions,” Malik added. “For MRVL specifically, our AI ASIC cuts reflect our concerns that C2026 [Amazon] Trainium volumes may be below our prior expectations as the company may not be the only ASIC provider for future Trainium products.” Malik maintained his Buy rating on Marvell, however.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Steel Partners to delist shares from NYSE
data.description:
• Steel Partners Holdings (NYSE:SPLP) has given formal notice to the NYSE of its intention to voluntarily delist its (a) Common units, no par value and (b) 6.0% Series A Preferred Units from the NYSE and SEC deregistration.
  • Following the de-listing from the NYSE, SPLP expects to quote the Common Units and Series A Units on the OTCQX platform.
  • The last day of trading in SPLP’s Common Units on the NYSE will be on or about May 1, 2025.
